summary: An excellent matrix recording captures a superb 1977 show highlighted by a soaring Sugaree, epic Good Lovin', and a marathon Playing in the Band.
review: The matrix blend of soundboard and audience creates an immersive, almost “human” listening experience with crystal‑clear highs and just enough studio‑like swish to add character. Musically the night is a high point for ’77 – Bertha, Good Lovin', Sugaree, Brown Eyed Women, Candyman and a one‑hour Playing in the Band are repeatedly praised, while only minor level hiccups on Samson are noted.

ai_rating.rationale: All five reviewers give 4–5 stars and consistently praise both the performance (standout songs, tight rhythm section) and the matrix recording quality despite a few minor quirks.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, immersive mix that blends SBD clarity with audience ambience; listeners note a pleasant “swishy” high‑frequency character from the matrix blend and occasional brief pauses between tracks, but overall the sound is clear and engaging.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Set 2 starts a bit rocky (level issues on Samson) but quickly launches into a series of classic ’77 moments; transitions are tight—especially Good Lovin' into Sugaree—and the hour‑long Playing in the Band serves as an epic centerpiece.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia delivers fine vocals and sweet, soaring guitar solos; a slight level issue is noted on Samson.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann and Mickey Hart lock in perfectly with a half‑time groove that drives the jams.
